channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Can taking a multivitamin daily help slow biological ageing?
And the Oscar goes to… Who picks the winners?
Word of the Week is Strait of Hormuz... The most important oil chokepoint in the world
This Week in Explainers: How the West Asia war has led to the largest oil supply disruptions in history
2 weeks of Iran war: Major takeaways as peace remains out of bounds
Inside West Asia’s escalating war: How will it end? An expert explains...
How Israel could be the only winner in the Iran war
item.description: Israel appears to be the only player that entered the conflict with a clear objective, and the only one that may derive any tangible gains from it

item.title: How China’s cautious diplomacy in West Asia reveals a broader strategy
item.description: Beijing wants to avoid being drawn into a confrontation with Washington while still demonstrating that it has an independent voice on major international crises

item.title: How the Iran war is acquiring global dimensions
item.description: What began as a regional confrontation is steadily transforming into a test of the international order itself that is challenging alliances, disrupting markets and forcing major powers to reconsider their strategic assumptions

item.title: Pramit Pal Chaudhuri: US transactionalism on India not a problem, I’m actually happy Landau made that statement
item.description: Corporate America has stuck with India, and even on immigration I’d argue the India story continues without too much trouble, says Pal Chaudhuri of Eurasia Group

item.title: How French archives in Pondicherry are helping India recover stolen temple idols
item.description: The decades-old photographic archives from two French institutes documenting South Indian temples have become critical evidence in proving the provenance of stolen idols and helping India bring its lost antiquities home

item.title: How Hormuz crisis exposes global complacency on maritime chokepoints
item.description: Energy crises often spark talk of diversification and strategic reserves, but prices eventually stabilise and supply routes resume. Markets call this risk, history calls it a habit
